question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `params.getReceiver()` as `Address.ZERO` to force `isContractCreate = true` in `execute()`, causing the service to simulate a contract creation instead of the actual historical contract call, producing a completely different execution trace than what was recorded in Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a `ContractDebugParameters` with non-empty `ethereumData` to force the `buildEthereumTransactionBody()` path in `execute()`, bypassing the `isContractCreate` check and simulating an Ethereum transaction instead of the actual Hedera-native transaction that was recorded in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildContractCallTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `gas` value (via `estimatedGas`) that is significantly higher than the actual gas limit used in the historical transaction, causing `buildContractCallTransactionBody()` to simulate execution with more gas and potentially reach different code paths than what actually executed in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildContractCallTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `gas` value of zero or near-zero to `buildContractCallTransactionBody()`, causing the simulated transaction to run out of gas immediately and produce an OOG trace that diverges from the actual successful execution recorded in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildContractCreateTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded `CONTRACT_CREATE_TX_FEES = 1_000_000_000L` in `buildContractCreateTransactionBody()` to simulate a contract creation with a fee that differs from the actual historical fee, causing the executor to evaluate fee-dependent logic differently and produce a divergent trace?
